# Implementation Plan: [FEATURE]

**Branch**: `004-anonymous-voting` | **Date**: 2026-03-16 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/004-anonymous-voting/spec.md`

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit.plan` on 2026-03-16.

## Summary

Allow anonymous (unauthenticated) users to submit votes on existing polls, with IP-based deduplication
(one vote per IP per poll) enforced server-side via SHA256 hashing. Vote changes are handled through
UPSERT semantics: if a prior vote exists for the same hashed-IP + poll, the submission replaces it;
otherwise it is inserted as new. The hashed IP is computed and stored exclusively on the server using
service-role credentials; the anon client never sees or handles IP-derived values.
Results are reflected within в‰¤ 5 seconds via the existing short-poll refresh mechanism, with interval
reduced to в‰¤ 5000 ms.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.x (strict mode)
**Primary Dependencies**: Next.js 15 App Router, React 19, Supabase JS v2, Zod v3, Node.js `crypto` (built-in)
**Storage**: Supabase Postgres with RLS; `vote_responses` table already has `voter_fingerprint text NOT NULL` + `UNIQUE(vote_id, voter_fingerprint)` constraint
**Testing**: Vitest with jsdom, `tests/unit/`, `tests/integration/`, `tests/contract/`
**Target Platform**: Docker + Vercel; existing `Dockerfile` and `docker-compose.yml` unchanged
**Project Type**: Next.js full-stack web app вЂ” server-side API routes handle all sensitive operations
**Performance Goals**: vote submission p95 < 300ms; result refresh в‰¤ 5 s (env `RESULT_TOKEN_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS` default reduced from 15 000 ms to 5 000 ms)
**Constraints**:
- RLS enforced on all vote tables; `voter_fingerprint` column must NEVER be readable by anon role
- `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` required (currently optional in env.ts вЂ” must become required with clear error)
- No application-level rate limiting; rely on Supabase platform throttling
- SHA256(IP) used for deduplication (accepted trade-off: brute-forceable against IPv4 space)
**Scale/Scope**: Small-to-medium concurrent load; single Supabase project

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Checked before Phase 0 research. Re-evaluated after Phase 1 design.*

### Pre-Design Gate (Pass / Fail / Deferred)

| Gate | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| TypeScript Contract Gate (MANDATORY) | **PASS** | New `VoteSubmissionSchema` Zod contract + `VoteSubmissionResponse` type to be added to `src/types/contracts.ts`; `VoteResponse` type extended with `voterFingerprint` (server-only). Frontend sends only `selectedOptionIds` вЂ” no IP fields. |
| Supabase Security Gate (MANDATORY) | **PASS** | `voter_fingerprint` column already exists with UNIQUE constraint. Service-role client handles all `vote_responses` read/write. Anon role has NO SELECT policy on `vote_responses` (column privacy preserved). No new RLS policies required. `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` will be promoted from optional to required. |
| Test Gate (MANDATORY) | **PASS** | Unit tests: `hashClientIp`, IP extraction logic, UPSERT path branching. Integration tests: POST `/api/votes/[voteId]/responses` (new+change+same+closed+invalid). Contract test: `VoteSubmissionSchema` validation. |
| Observability Gate (RECOMMENDED) | **PASS** | Structured logs with `correlationId` on every submit/change outcome using existing `logVoteRequest`/`logVoteFailure` pattern; hashed IP and raw IP excluded from all log entries. |
| Docker Reproducibility Gate (RECOMMENDED) | **PASS** | No Dockerfile change required; feature validated through existing `docker-compose.yml` build flow. |
| Performance Gate (RECOMMENDED) | **PASS** | Submission p95 < 300 ms (single UPSERT); refresh interval reduced to в‰¤ 5 000 ms via `RESULT_TOKEN_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS` default change. |

### Post-Design Re-evaluation

| Gate | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| TypeScript Contract Gate (MANDATORY) | **PASS** | `VoteSubmissionSchema` + `VoteSubmissionResponse` defined in `contracts/vote-submit.schema.ts`; to be merged into `src/types/contracts.ts`. No IP-derived fields in client-facing types. |
| Supabase Security Gate (MANDATORY) | **PASS** | No new RLS policies needed; service-role used for all `vote_responses` ops; anon SELECT remains blocked; `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` promoted to required. |
| Test Gate (MANDATORY) | **PASS** | Test files identified: `vote-ip-hash.test.ts` (unit), `vote-submit.integration.test.ts` (integration), `vote-submit.contract.test.ts` (contract). |

**Structure Decision**: Single Next.js project. Vote submission API lives at
`app/api/votes/[voteId]/responses/route.ts` (POST only), keeping GET on the existing
`[voteId]/route.ts` route unchanged. All IP-sensitive operations are isolated to
`src/lib/vote/ip.ts` and handled only in server-side route handlers.

## Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations. No Deadline Waivers required.

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| Service-role for all `vote_responses` operations | Prevents anon client from reading `voter_fingerprint` via Supabase REST API; bypasses RLS cleanly for server-side UPSERT |
| IP-only SHA256 (new `ip.ts`, not composite `fingerprint.ts`) | Spec requires per-IP (not per-device) deduplication; composite fingerprint would allow same IP + different UA to bypass deduplication |
| Single POST endpoint with UPSERT semantics | Eliminates need for separate "change" endpoint; client sends same payload shape for both new and change flows |
| `RESULT_TOKEN_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS` default: 15 000 ms в†’ 5 000 ms | Existing default violates spec SC-003 (в‰¤5 s refresh); no Supabase Realtime complexity needed |
| No application-level rate limiting | Accepted trade-off per spec clarification; Supabase platform throttling + DB uniqueness constraint is sufficient protection at current scale |
